title: Chapter 1 Master Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of Python basics, printing, comments, and errors.

questions.question: Who created the Python programming language?
questions.explanation: Guido van Rossum created Python in 1991.

questions.question: Python is an interpreted language.
questions.explanation: Python code is executed line by line by the interpreter, without a separate compilation step.

questions.question: What function is used to display text on the screen in Python?
questions.explanation: print() is Python's built-in function for displaying output.

questions.question: Which of these is a valid Python string?
questions.explanation: Strings must be enclosed in matching quotes. 'Hello' uses valid single quotes.

questions.question: In Python, 'Hello' and "Hello" produce the same result.
questions.explanation: Both single and double quotes create identical strings in Python.

questions.question: What happens when you run: Print('Hello')?
questions.explanation: Python is case-sensitive. 'Print' is not the same as 'print', causing a NameError.

questions.question: Comments in Python start with the _____ symbol.
questions.explanation: Python uses the hash symbol (#) for single-line comments.

questions.question: Comments are executed by the Python interpreter.
questions.explanation: Comments are completely ignored by the interpreter. They're meant only for human readers.

questions.question: What does the sep parameter in print() control?
questions.explanation: The sep parameter determines what character(s) separate multiple items in a print() call.

questions.question: What is the output of print('A', 'B', sep='-')?
questions.explanation: The sep='-' parameter replaces the default space separator with a dash.

questions.question: What does the escape character \n do?
questions.explanation: \n is the newline escape character — it moves output to the next line.

questions.question: What type of error is: print('Hello)?
questions.explanation: Missing the closing quote creates a SyntaxError — Python can't parse the unterminated string.

questions.question: What does the end parameter in print() do?
options: Controls what is printed after the output
questions.explanation: The end parameter specifies what character(s) come after the printed text. Default is a newline.

questions.question: Python uses curly braces {} to define code blocks.
questions.explanation: Python uses indentation (whitespace) to define code blocks, not curly braces.

questions.question: Which error occurs when you use a variable that hasn't been defined?
questions.explanation: A NameError is raised when Python encounters a name it doesn't recognize.

questions.question: What is 'commenting out' code?
options: Adding a # to temporarily disable code
questions.explanation: Commenting out means adding # to disable code without deleting it — useful for debugging.

questions.question: The _____ reads Python code line by line and executes it.
questions.explanation: Python uses an interpreter that reads and executes code line by line.

questions.question: Which escape character adds a tab space?
questions.explanation: \t is the tab escape character, adding a larger indent space.

questions.question:
What is the output of: print('Hello', end='!')?
print('World')
questions.explanation: end='!' replaces the default newline with '!', so 'World' prints right after on the same line.

questions.question: Python file extensions end with .py
questions.explanation: Python source code files use the .py extension.

questions.question: Where should you look first when reading a Python error traceback?
questions.explanation: The error type and description are at the bottom of the traceback.

questions.question: Python was named after the comedy group Monty _____.
questions.explanation: Python was named after the British comedy group Monty Python, not the snake!
